description: Copies a vector or line to a new starting point, preserving its direction and magnitude.
utility: Copy a vector to start at a new position while preserving direction and magnitude

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: The vector or line to copy

arguments.name: point
arguments.description: The new starting point for the copied vector

alternates.description: Copy vector to start at the given point
Copy vector to start at (x, y)
Copy line as a vector starting at the given point

examples.scenario: Copy a vector to a new starting point
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 2))
V2 = placeat(G, V, point(G, 1, 1))

examples.scenario: Copy a vector to explicit coordinates
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 3, 2))
V2 = placeat(G, V, 2, 3)

examples.scenario: Build a parallelogram by placing vectors at tips
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
A = vector(G, O, point(G, 4, 0))
B = vector(G, O, point(G, 1, 3))
A2 = placeat(G, A, point(G, 1, 3))
B2 = placeat(G, B, point(G, 4, 0))

examples.scenario: Place a line as a vector at a new position
examples.expression:
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 3, 0)
L = line(G, A, B)
V = placeat(G, L, point(G, 0, 4))

examples.scenario: Compare equivalent vectors at different positions
examples.expression:
O = point(G, 0, 0)
V = vector(G, O, point(G, 2, 3))
V1 = placeat(G, V, point(G, 3, 0))
V2 = placeat(G, V, point(G, 6, 0))
